releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Changelog

## 0.9.9

Maintenance release. No functional changes — internal cleanups to comply with the Obsidian plugin guidelines.

### If you're updating from before 0.9.8

Carried over from 0.9.8: the plugin now signs in to Garmin with official sign-in tokens instead of a browser session, so your connection stays alive far longer and refreshes itself in the background. **You'll need to log in to Garmin once** after updating — after that it just keeps going. (Full details in the 0.9.8 notes below.)

### Changed

- Raised the minimum required Obsidian version to 1.5.4. The plugin already relied on APIs introduced in that version (`Vault.createFolder`), so this only makes the existing requirement explicit.
- Internal: use `activeDocument` and `window.setTimeout` instead of the global `document` / `setTimeout` for popout-window compatibility.

## 0.9.8

This update changes how the plugin signs in to Garmin. Instead of a browser session that quietly expired after a couple of hours, it now uses Garmin's official sign-in tokens — so your connection stays alive far longer and keeps refreshing itself in the background.

### What changed for you

- **You stay connected much longer.** The old browser session expired after a few hours, which is what caused the recurring "auto-sync paused" messages. Now you sign in once and the plugin quietly renews the connection on its own.
- **A one-time sign-in.** Because the sign-in method changed, you'll need to log in to Garmin once after installing this update. After that it just keeps going.
- **Clearer messages.** If Garmin ever genuinely needs you to sign in again, the plugin shows a clear message with a "Log in again" button instead of silently pausing.
- **Fewer false alarms.** Temporary Garmin or network hiccups no longer pause auto-sync — the plugin waits and retries on its own.

### Privacy note

To keep you signed in, the plugin stores Garmin sign-in tokens locally in Obsidian's plugin data. It does not store your Garmin password. Use **Logout** in the plugin settings to clear them on this device.

## 0.9.7

This update makes Garmin auto-sync more reliable when Garmin sessions expire or need to be refreshed.

### Improved

- Auto-sync now checks first whether there is actually anything to sync before touching the Garmin session. This avoids unnecessary login/session checks while everything is still in cooldown.
- If Garmin requires a fresh login, auto-sync now pauses with a clear persistent message and a direct "Log in again" button.
- Auto-sync no longer opens a surprise Garmin login window in the background while you are just opening a daily note.
- Saved Garmin browser sessions are restored more reliably, so you should need to log in less often.

### Fixed

- Fixed cases where expired Garmin cookies could make the plugin think the session was still fresh.
- Fixed a race condition during login that could incorrectly report a login timeout.
- Reduced false auto-sync pauses when only some Garmin endpoints temporarily reject a request.

### Privacy note

Garmin session cookies may be stored locally in Obsidian's plugin data so the plugin can restore your session. The plugin does not store your Garmin password. Use **Logout** in the plugin settings to clear the saved Garmin session on this device.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Garmin Health Sync 0.9.6

This release stabilizes the plugin for Obsidian's plugin submission portal and fixes the Garmin sync issues reported after `0.9.6-beta.1`.

### Fixed

- Fixed partial Garmin direct fetch results where `steps`, `resting_hr`, `stress`, `calories_active`, and `intensity_min` could be missing even though other data imported correctly.
- Hardened Garmin display name detection so path fragments such as `v2` are no longer stored as the user identifier.
- Fixed **Sync current note** so it syncs the active Daily Note date directly, including today's note.
- Improved frontmatter writes to avoid unnecessary updates and preserve existing note content more safely.

### Added

- Added optional extended `vo2_max` metric in settings.

### Release

- Published as stable `0.9.6` with matching `manifest.json`, `versions.json`, and GitHub release tag.
- Release assets: `main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css`.

releases.author: fcandi
releases.description:
## Summary

- **moment.js date formatting** — replaced the simple `YYYY/MM/DD` string replacement with Obsidian's built-in moment.js library. This supports all moment format tokens (`ddd`, `MMM`, `Wo`, `dddd`, etc.), matching the behavior of Obsidian's core Daily Notes plugin.

### Example

Format `YYYY-MM-DD-ddd` now correctly produces `2026-04-10-Thu` instead of leaving `ddd` as a literal string.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
releases.name: v0.9.6-beta.1 — moment.js date formatting

releases.author: fcandi
releases.description:
### New feature

- **Metric / Imperial unit system.** New dropdown in settings to choose between metric (km, kg) and imperial (mi, lbs). Affects distance, weight, activity display strings, and machine-readable training data. All 6 languages supported.

### How it works

- Internal data pipeline always uses SI units from the Garmin API (meters, grams)
- Conversion to imperial happens at output time, right before writing to frontmatter
- No risk of double conversion — Garmin API always returns SI regardless of app settings

### Switching units on existing data

If you switch from metric to imperial and re-sync (manual or backfill), the new keys (`distance_mi`, `weight_lbs`) will be written. Old keys (`distance_km`, `weight_kg`) remain as orphaned properties — they can be manually removed if desired.

### Full changelog
https://github.com/fcandi/Garmin-Health-Sync/compare/0.9.4...0.9.5
releases.name: v0.9.5-beta.4 — Imperial unit system

releases.author: fcandi
releases.description:
### Bug fix

- **Auto-sync now correctly fetches workouts/activities.** The Garmin daily-summary page doesn't trigger the activities API call, so workouts were silently missing during auto-sync (especially on first sync after Obsidian startup when the CSRF token wasn't yet captured). After page navigation, any missing required endpoints are now supplemented via direct fetch.

### Full changelog
https://github.com/fcandi/Garmin-Health-Sync/compare/0.9.3...0.9.4
releases.name: v0.9.4-beta.3 — Fix: auto-sync activities

releases.author: fcandi
releases.description:
## Changes since v0.9.4-beta.1

- **Usage section in all READMEs** — dedicated section explaining auto-sync, manual sync, and backfill with step-by-step Command Palette instructions (EN, DE, ZH, JA, ES, FR)

## Included from beta.1

- **China server region support** — users on `garmin.cn` can now select their server region in settings
releases.name: v0.9.4-beta.2 — China server + usage docs

releases.author: fcandi
releases.description:
## China server region support (testing)

This pre-release adds a **server region** setting for Chinese users whose Garmin accounts are registered at `garmin.cn`.

### How to test

1. Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` from this release
2. Copy them to `.obsidian/plugins/garmin-health-sync/` in your vault
3. Restart Obsidian
4. Go to **Settings → Garmin Health Sync**
5. Change **Server region** to **China (garmin.cn)**
6. Log in with your garmin.cn account

### What changed

- New setting: Server region (International / China)
- All API endpoints switch between `garmin.com` and `garmin.cn`
- SSO login URL switches to `sso.garmin.cn`
- Translations in 6 languages (EN, DE, ZH, JA, ES, FR)

Please report any issues! 🧪
releases.name: v0.9.4-beta.1 — China server support

releases.author: fcandi
releases.description:
### Fixes
- Login timeout fix: increased BrowserWindow timeout to prevent premature auth failures
- Global timeouts: added configurable timeout settings for all API requests  
- Fetch timeout: individual fetch operations now respect timeout configuration
releases.name: v0.9.2 — login timeout fix, global timeouts, fetch timeout

releases.author: fcandi
releases.description:
## What's New

- **Fix: nested daily note paths** — Formats like `YYYY/MM/YYYY-MM-DD` now work correctly. Subdirectories are created automatically.
- **Fix: duplicate frontmatter keys** — Corrupt YAML (duplicate keys from previous syncs) is detected and repaired automatically.
- **Smart Re-Sync** — Data within the last 72h is refreshed automatically (30 min after first sync, then every 6h). Late Garmin watch syncs are picked up without manual action.
- **HRV: per-night value** — Uses `lastNightAvg` instead of weekly average for meaningful day-to-day variation.
- **Removed: Hydration metric** — Removed since it requires manual input and isn't sensor-tracked.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/fcandi/obsidian-health-sync/compare/0.9.0...0.9.1
releases.name: v0.9.1 — nested daily note paths, YAML dedup, smart re-sync

releases.author: fcandi
releases.description:
## Health Sync v0.9.0 — Beta Release

First public beta. Sync health data from Garmin Connect into your Obsidian Daily Notes.

### Features
- 20+ health metrics (steps, sleep, HRV, stress, body battery, SpO2, weight, ...)
- Activity tracking with human-readable summaries
- Workout location via reverse geocoding
- Auto-sync last 7 days on Obsidian startup
- Backfill historical data
- Recursive Daily Note search (subdirectories supported)
- Activity key normalization for cross-provider compatibility
- Optional machine-readable `trainings` field for Dataview
- Multi-language UI (EN, DE, ZH, JA, ES, FR)

### Installation
1. Download `main.js` and `manifest.json`
2. Create `.obsidian/plugins/obsidian-health-sync/` in your vault
3. Copy both files into that folder
4. Enable in Settings → Community Plugins
5. Log in to Garmin Connect in plugin settings

### Known Limitations
- Desktop only (requires Electron BrowserWindow)
- Garmin Connect only (more providers planned)
releases.name: v0.9.0 — Beta Release
